lede: After your international flight arrives, follow signs to Passport Control for entry processing. Then, continue to the international baggage claim area. Global Entry opens in a new tab members can use paper-free kiosks for expedited entry. Apply to become a Global Entry opens in a new tab member, apply on the Customs and Border Protection website. U.S Customs and Border Protection also offers Enrollment on Arrival opens in a new tab where applicants can complete their Global Entry interview on arrival to JFK. U.S. citizens and Canadian citizens may use the free Mobile Passport Control (MPC) app opens in a new tab—no pre-approval needed. Travelers from other countries should visit the Customs and Border Protection website opens in a new tab for full immigration and customs details.

paragraphs: Whether you are looking for food, shopping, or services, or you're ready to begin your adventure, explore our interactive map opens in a new tab to find your way around JFK Airport.

links.text: interactive map opens in a new tab

paragraphs: After your international flight arrives, follow signs to Passport Control for entry processing. Then, continue to the international baggage claim area.
Passport Control wait times are updated throughout the day. Wait times are not available for other terminals.

links.text: Global Entry opens in a new tab
Enrollment on Arrival opens in a new tab
Mobile Passport Control (MPC) app opens in a new tab
Customs and Border Protection website opens in a new tab

paragraphs: Whether you’re ready to explore New York or heading straight to your next flight, JFK offers multiple ways to get to your next destination.
New York has something for everyone, from iconic landmarks to one-of-a-kind experiences. Hop off the plane and explore all the state has to offer!

h3s: Grab a snack, pick up a gift, or take a break
Find carts, cash, and baggage help
Airport hotels make late arrivals easier
paragraphs: If you’re changing planes at JFK or traveling to another airport nearby, the Connections Guide has helpful tips to make your trip easier.
Need a ride from JFK? Learn about taxis, shuttles, rental cars, public transit, and parking options to help you get where you’re going.
Before you leave JFK, stop by for a quick bite, grab a last-minute gift, or relax at one of our lounges or shops.
After you arrive at JFK, you can find luggage carts, ATMs, porter services, and help with baggage issues. For lost or delayed bags, contact your airline first, then visit a baggage office if needed.
Take it easy after a late arrival at JFK, and book a stay at an airport hotel.

links.text: Go to transportation and parking info
